permissions.item: You have permission to read, write, and edit any files in this project
No need to ask for approval before making code changes

development_commands.command: bun run build - Build for production
bun run check - Run lint and TypeScript checks
bun run test - Run jest tests

project_notes.description: This is a React/TypeScript NAO (Network of Authentic Others) community management application with features for:

features.feature: Contact management with NAO membership status

component.name: IconButton
component.description: Standardized circular icon container with consistent sizing and theming

variants.variant: Primary blue theme for vouch count display
Pink theme for praise count display
Blue theme for NAO membership status
Gray theme for contact source (LinkedIn, etc.)
Dynamic color theme for relationship categories

notes.note: All icons automatically get consistent sizing and circular backgrounds
Count prop adds a small badge with the number
Category variant allows custom backgroundColor and color props
Use this component for all contact box icons to maintain consistency

general_instructions.instruction: Never do manual kludges, workarounds, hacks or "just to make it work" code
Always completely implement any functionality as requested
Always use the most up to date official documentation for libraries or techniques
Prefer zustand stores above useState when we have more than 3 useState
Use LDO and nextgraphauth.session for persistent data (contacts for example), consider this to be our "database"

Be sure to run `bun run test` and verify that you haven't broken any tests, verify with the user, if necessary, that the fault is in the code or in the test and that the breaking change was intentional or not.

component_structure.rule: Try to keep below 150 lines per component file
One component per file with named exports
Each component gets its own directory with index.ts
Separate concerns: presentation, logic, data, styling
Use composition over large monolithic components

file_organization.structure: src/ components/ feature-name/ ComponentName/ index.ts (exports) ComponentName.tsx (component) types.ts (local types) stores/ featureStore.ts (Zustand stores) hooks/ useFeatureName.ts (custom hooks) types/ feature.ts (shared types)

state_management_rules.rule: Use Zustand stores instead of useState when >3 state variables
LDO + nextgraphauth.session for persistent data (contacts, groups, etc.)
Custom hooks for complex logic and data fetching
No business logic in presentation components

component_patterns.pattern: Functional components with explicit TypeScript interfaces
Extract reusable UI components to shared components/ui/
Use custom hooks for data fetching and complex state logic
Composition pattern: combine small components rather than building large ones

anti_patterns.anti_pattern: Using array indices as React keys
Large return statements with everything inline

required_practices.practice: Every iterative display must have proper key= attribute
Avoid array index as key for dynamic lists
Explicit TypeScript interfaces for all props
Follow all ESLint rules without exceptions

code_style.style_rule: Use react hooks: useMemo, useCallback, and other standard hooks
Ensure iterative displays have a key= attribute (for..of, map, etc)
Avoid array index as or within key= for iterative displays (for..of, map, etc)
TypeScript with strict typing and explicit type annotations
Imports: Organized imports with path aliases (@/components, @/hooks, etc.)
Formatting: eslint & prettier with 2-space indentation, 100 char line width
Components: Functional components with named exports
Naming: PascalCase for components/types, camelCase for variables/functions
Comments: No comments about changes or removals, only absolutely necessary comments in any case
YOU MUST FOLLOW ALL ESLINT RULES, NO EXCEPTIONS

absolute_rules.rule_1: Follow patterns, styles, practices, rules and other instructions found in the CLAUDE.xml file Display all absolute_rules at the start of every response.
